critical_errors: Includes a forbidden distractor title verbatim: "Nano-optical imaging of the tailored exciton-polariton transport".

The top claimed reference, "Signatures of fractional charges via anyon-trions in twisted MoTe2," is not present in the supplied context excerpt, so its detailed claims and local paths are unsupported here.
Claims about graph links for arXiv 2602.04561, such as observable:pl and observable:rmcd, go beyond the supplied node text.
missing_key_points: A stricter answer should avoid naming forbidden polariton/waveguide titles verbatim and simply say those polariton/waveguide distractors are excluded.
The answer should more clearly limit itself to references actually evidenced in the context, especially arXiv 2602.04561 and any provided local paper shelves.
summary: The answer is relevant and correctly applies the exclusion logic in spirit, but it overreaches beyond the supplied context by elevating an unsupported anyon-trion paper as the strongest hit and by asserting local paths and graph links not shown. It also violates the forbidden-term constraint by naming a distractor title verbatim. The caveat about incomplete context is helpful, but faithfulness and citation discipline are weak.
